white.$description: Pure white; base for glass borders, control borders, and overlay compositions

teal-500.$description: Maycation brand teal; primary accent in dark mode

teal-950.$description: Near-black teal; darkest point of the teal scale. Used as text on teal accent backgrounds

blue-500.$description: Blue; used for travel kind badge and editor role pill

blue-vivid.$description: Apple System Blue; used for countdown labels, trip intel header, and completed planner item borders. Distinct from blue-500 — more saturated, carries temporal/milestone emphasis

amber-500.$description: Amber; used for reservation kind badge, attention state, and expired invite

purple-500.$description: Lavender-purple; used for note kind badge and viewer role pill

red-500.$description: Muted red; used for danger state borders

red-100.$description: Soft pink; lightest red. Readable on dark surfaces; used as danger message text

neutral-100.$description: Near-white; primary text, headings, strong labels

neutral-250.$description: Silver-gray; icon resting state before selection. Sits between neutral-100 (primary) and neutral-200 (secondary). Used by IconButton --complete unselected state.

neutral-700.$description: Dark border; opaque border for non-glass components

neutral-800.$description: Near-black; base color for glass morphism surfaces before opacity is applied

neutral-850.$description: Control surface background in dark mode

neutral-950.$description: Deepest background; retained as a primitive for legacy reference

default.$description: Fallback panel background; used before glass morphism is applied

input.$description: Form input background. CSS composite: color.primitive.black × opacity.primitive.28. Do not use element-level opacity — use rgba() directly so child text remains fully opaque. Figma: bind fill color to Color/Black, fill opacity to Opacity/Surface/Input.

glass.$description: Glass morphism background for cards, panels, modals. CSS composite: color.primitive.neutral-800 × opacity.primitive.72. Do not use element-level opacity — use rgba() directly so child text remains fully opaque. Figma: bind fill color to Color/Neutral 800, fill opacity to Opacity/Surface/Glass.

badge.$description: Badge background fill. CSS composite: color.primitive.black × opacity.primitive.22. Intentionally off-scale (not 20 or 28) — value chosen for correct Badge visual weight. Do not use element-level opacity. Figma: bind fill color to Color/Black, fill opacity to Opacity/Surface/Badge.

feedback-neutral.$description: FeedbackMessage neutral background fill. CSS composite: color.primitive.white × opacity.primitive.5. Intentionally off-scale — 5% is the minimum effective opacity for a visible white surface on a near-black background. Do not use element-level opacity. Figma: bind fill color to Color/White, fill opacity to Opacity/Surface/FeedbackNeutral.

glass.$description: Subtle white edge for glass surfaces. CSS composite: color.primitive.white × opacity.primitive.8. Figma: bind stroke color to Color/White, stroke opacity to Opacity/Border/Glass.

control.$description: Border for interactive control elements; form inputs, select, textarea, role/status/progress pills, secondary buttons. CSS composite: color.primitive.white × opacity.primitive.10. Intentionally brighter than border.glass to signal interactivity. Figma: bind stroke color to Color/White, stroke opacity to Opacity/Border/Control.

primary.$description: Headings, strong text, labels on dark backgrounds

default.$description: Brand accent; used on eyebrows, interactive focus rings, hover borders

text.$description: Text color for use on accent-colored backgrounds

surface.$description: Danger action background fill. CSS composite: color.primitive.red-500 × opacity.primitive.20. Figma: bind fill color to Color/Red 500, fill opacity to Opacity/Danger/Surface.

text.$description: Danger state text; readable on dark surfaces

surface.$description: Success state background fill. CSS composite: color.primitive.teal-500 × opacity.primitive.8. Do not use element-level opacity — use rgba() directly so child text remains fully opaque. Figma: bind fill color to Color/Teal 500, fill opacity to Opacity/Success/Surface.

border.$description: Success state border. CSS composite: color.primitive.teal-500 × opacity.primitive.30. Figma: bind stroke color to Color/Teal 500, stroke opacity to Opacity/Success/Border.

text.$description: Success state text. Teal 500 fully opaque — same hue as accent. Used for text inside FeedbackMessage --success.

border.$description: Info UI state border. Semantic alias for Blue 500. Used by Badge --info tone. Do not use color.role.editor for component UI states — role tokens carry product-domain meaning.

Warning UI state border. Semantic alias for Amber 500. Used by Badge --warning and ProgressPill --attention tones. Do not use color.kind.reservation for component UI states — kind tokens carry product-domain meaning.
Secondary UI state border. Semantic alias for Purple 500. Used by Badge --secondary tone. Do not use color.role.viewer for component UI states — role tokens carry product-domain meaning.

complete.$description: Icon color for the IconButton --complete variant in its unselected (resting) state. Distinct from text.primary (#f5f7fb) and text.secondary (#b6bfcc) — the intermediate silver signals 'checkable but not yet checked'. Do not use text.* tokens for this; icon state color is a separate semantic concern.

activity.$description: Activity planner item kind badge border; shares the brand accent color

travel.$description: Travel planner item kind badge border

reservation.$description: Reservation planner item kind badge border

note.$description: Note planner item kind badge border

owner.$description: Owner role pill border; shares the brand accent color

default.$description: Modal backdrop overlay. CSS composite: color.primitive.black × opacity.primitive.78. Applied to .modal-backdrop which is a separate full-screen element with no text children, so rgba() and element opacity are both acceptable here.
Countdown and milestone label color; used on trip intel card header spans. Signals temporal anticipation and high-emphasis trip moments

border.$description: Completed planner item card border. CSS composite: color.primitive.blue-vivid × opacity.primitive.28. Signals a completed milestone without overwhelming the card.
